    )
    if CORRUPT_COL not in df.columns:
        df = df.withColumn(CORRUPT_COL, F.lit(None).cast("string"))
    df = df.cache()
    df.count()  # materialize so _corrupt_record is usable in downstream transforms

    cols = df.columns
    id_col = pick(cols, ["order_id", "orderid", "id", "oid", "order"])
    amt_col = pick(cols, ["amount", "amt", "total", "price", "value", "revenue", "gross"])
    cat_col = pick(cols, ["category", "cat", "segment", "type", "product_category"])
    ts_col = pick(
        cols,
        [
            "event_time", "event_ts", "event_timestamp", "timestamp", "ts",
            "time", "created_at", "order_time", "order_ts", "datetime",
            "occurred_at", "date", "event_date",
        ],
    )

    corrupt = F.col(CORRUPT_COL)

    # --- order_id (salvage from corrupt raw text if needed) -------------------
    salvaged_id = F.regexp_extract(
        F.coalesce(corrupt, F.lit("")),
        r'"order[_]?id"\s*:\s*"?([^",}\s]+)',
        1,
    )
    salvaged_id = F.when(salvaged_id == "", None).otherwise(salvaged_id)
    if id_col is not None:
        order_id = F.coalesce(F.col(id_col).cast("string"), salvaged_id)
    else:
        order_id = salvaged_id
    order_id = F.when(F.trim(order_id) == "", None).otherwise(F.trim(order_id))

    # --- amount (scrub inconsistent formatting, never throw) ------------------
    if amt_col is not None:
        amt_str = F.col(amt_col).cast("string")
        amt_clean = F.regexp_replace(amt_str, r"[^0-9.\-]", "")
        amt_clean = F.when(amt_clean.rlike(r"^-?\d+(\.\d+)?$"), amt_clean).otherwise(None)
        amount = amt_clean.cast("double")
    else:
        amount = F.lit(None).cast("double")

    # --- category -------------------------------------------------------------
    if cat_col is not None:
        category = F.col(cat_col).cast("string")
        category = F.when(F.trim(category) == "", None).otherwise(F.trim(category))
    else:
        category = F.lit(None).cast("string")

    # --- timestamp -> UTC event_date (handle ISO + epoch s / ms) --------------
    if ts_col is not None:
        ts_raw = F.col(ts_col).cast("string")
        is_num = ts_raw.rlike(r"^\d+$")
        epoch_long = ts_raw.cast("long")
        secs = F.when(F.length(ts_raw) >= 13, (epoch_long / F.lit(1000.0)).cast("long")).otherwise(epoch_long)
        ts_num = F.timestamp_seconds(secs)
        ts_iso = F.coalesce(
            F.to_timestamp(ts_raw),
            F.to_timestamp(ts_raw, "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss'Z'"),
            F.to_timestamp(ts_raw, "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss"),
            F.to_timestamp(ts_raw, "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"),
            F.to_timestamp(ts_raw, "yyyy-MM-dd"),
        )
        event_ts = F.when(is_num, ts_num).otherwise(ts_iso)
    else:
        event_ts = F.lit(None).cast("timestamp")

    event_date = F.to_date(event_ts)

    stage = df.select(
        order_id.alias("order_id"),
        amount.alias("amount"),
        category.alias("category"),
        event_ts.alias("event_ts"),
        event_date.alias("event_date"),
    )

    # plausibility of the calendar day (excludes impossible / far-future dates)
    valid_date = (
        F.col("event_date").isNotNull()
        & (F.col("event_date") >= F.lit("2000-01-01").cast("date"))
        & (F.col("event_date") <= F.current_date())
    )
    stage = stage.withColumn("valid_date", valid_date)

    # keep only records we can key on (corrupt order_id-less lines cannot be kept)
    stage = stage.filter(F.col("order_id").isNotNull())

    # --- deterministic de-duplication to one canonical row per order ----------
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(
        F.col("amount").isNull().asc(),        # prefer rows that carry an amount
        F.col("valid_date").desc(),            # prefer a plausible date
        F.col("event_ts").asc_nulls_last(),    # earliest plausible event
        F.col("amount").asc_nulls_last(),      # stable tie-breaks
        F.col("category").asc_nulls_last(),
    )
    canonical = (
        stage.withColumn("rn", F.row_number().over(w))
        .filter(F.col("rn") == 1)
        .drop("rn")
    )
    canonical = canonical.cache()
    canonical.count()

    # --- silver_orders: one row per order (corrupt lines survive here) --------
    silver = canonical.select("order_id", "amount", "category")

    # --- gold_daily: trustworthy per-UTC-day revenue, joinable to order detail
    gold_base = canonical.filter(F.col("valid_date") & F.col("amount").isNotNull())
    daily = Window.partitionBy("event_date")
    gold = (
        gold_base.withColumn("revenue", F.sum("amount").over(daily))
        .select("event_date", "revenue", "order_id", "amount", "category")
    )

    # --- write silver ---------------------------------------------------------
    if dedup_path:
        silver.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(dedup_path)
    else:
        silver.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable("silver_orders")

    # --- write gold (final contract output) -----------------------------------
    if out_path:
        gold.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(out_path)
    elif out_table:
        gold.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable(out_table)
    else:
        raise SystemExit("No output location provided (AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH / AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE)")

    print("Run is COMPLETED")
    spark.stop()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

"""
orders_silver_gold pipeline (imperative pyspark).

Reads a messy NDJSON orders feed and produces:
  * gold_daily  (event_date DATE [UTC], revenue, order_id, amount, category)  -- per (valid) order
  * silver_orders (order_id, amount, category)                                -- one row per order_id

Messiness handled:
  - duplicate orders (same order_id many times)  -> deterministic dedup
  - wrong/future event times                     -> dropped relative to current_date (far-future => stable)
  - inconsistently recorded amounts              -> normalized (currency symbols, thousands separators)
  - outright corrupt lines                        -> preserved in silver under a stable synthetic key,
                                                     excluded from gold revenue (must NOT vanish)

Reprocessing the same input yields the same correct result (deterministic ordering / tie-breaks).
"""
import os
import sys

from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F
from pyspark.sql import types as T
from pyspark.sql.window import Window

DEFAULT_INPUT = (
    "/home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/"
    ".work.powered.AB.n12/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed14142.ndjson"
)


def pick(cols, candidates):
    """Pick the first existing column matching candidate names (exact, then substring)."""
    lower = {c.lower(): c for c in cols}
    for cand in candidates:
        if cand in lower:
            return lower[cand]
    for c in cols:
        cl = c.lower()
        for cand in candidates:
            if cand in cl:
                return c
    return None


def get_spark():
    spark = (
        SparkSession.builder.appName("orders_silver_gold")
        .config("spark.sql.session.timeZone", "UTC")
        .config("spark.sql.legacy.timeParserPolicy", "LEGACY")
        .getOrCreate()
    )
    spark.conf.set("spark.sql.session.timeZone", "UTC")
    return spark


def main():
    spark = get_spark()

    input_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_INPUT_PATH") or DEFAULT_INPUT
    output_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH")
    output_table = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE")
    dedup_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_DEDUP_PATH")
    dedup_table = os.environ.get("AGENT_DEDUP_TABLE")

    # ---------------------------------------------------------------
    # 1) PROFILE: infer the raw schema (permissive read drops corrupt lines)
    # ---------------------------------------------------------------
    inferred = spark.read.json(input_path)
    fields = [f for f in inferred.schema.fields if f.name != "_corrupt_record"]
    print("=== inferred raw schema ===")
    for f in fields:
        print("  ", f.name, f.dataType.simpleString())
    cols = [f.name for f in fields]

    oid_field = pick(cols, ["order_id", "orderid", "oid", "order", "id"])
    amt_field = pick(cols, ["amount", "amt", "price", "total", "value", "revenue", "gross"])
    ts_field = pick(cols, ["event_time", "event_ts", "event_timestamp", "timestamp",
                           "ts", "time", "created_at", "order_time", "event_date", "date"])
    cat_field = pick(cols, ["category", "cat", "product_category", "type", "segment"])
    print("=== field mapping ===")
    print("  order_id =", oid_field, "| amount =", amt_field,
          "| time =", ts_field, "| category =", cat_field)

    # ---------------------------------------------------------------
    # 2) Re-read raw text so corrupt lines are visible (not silently dropped)
    # ---------------------------------------------------------------
    base_schema = T.StructType(fields)
    raw = spark.read.text(input_path).select(F.col("value").alias("raw_line"))
    parsed = raw.select(
        F.col("raw_line"),
        F.from_json(F.col("raw_line"), base_schema).alias("j"),
    )

    j = F.col("j")

    # --- order_id ---
    if oid_field:
        oid = F.trim(j[oid_field].cast("string"))
        oid = F.when(F.length(oid) > 0, oid)
    else:
        oid = F.lit(None).cast("string")
    is_corrupt = j.isNull() | oid.isNull()
    synth_id = F.concat(F.lit("__corrupt__"), F.sha2(F.col("raw_line"), 256))
    final_oid = F.coalesce(oid, synth_id)

    # --- amount (normalize messy encodings: $, commas, etc.) ---
    if amt_field:
        amt_raw = j[amt_field].cast("string")
        amt_clean = F.regexp_replace(amt_raw, "[^0-9.\\-]", "")
        amount = F.round(F.when(F.length(amt_clean) > 0, amt_clean.cast("double")), 2)
    else:
        amount = F.lit(None).cast("double")
    valid_amount = amount.isNotNull()

    # --- event timestamp -> UTC calendar day ---
    if ts_field:
        t_raw = j[ts_field].cast("string")
        is_num = t_raw.rlike("^[0-9]+$")
        epoch = t_raw.cast("long")
        ts_sec = epoch.cast("timestamp")
        ts_ms = (epoch / F.lit(1000.0)).cast("timestamp")
        ts_epoch = F.when(is_num,
                          F.when(F.length(t_raw) >= 12, ts_ms).otherwise(ts_sec))
        ts_str = F.coalesce(
            F.to_timestamp(t_raw),
            F.to_timestamp(t_raw, "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss'Z'"),
            F.to_timestamp(t_raw, "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss"),
            F.to_timestamp(t_raw, "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"),
            F.to_timestamp(t_raw, "yyyy-MM-dd"),
            F.to_timestamp(t_raw, "MM/dd/yyyy"),
        )
        event_ts = F.coalesce(ts_epoch, ts_str)
    else:
        event_ts = F.lit(None).cast("timestamp")
    event_date = F.to_date(event_ts)
    valid_time = (
        event_date.isNotNull()
        & (event_date <= F.current_date())
        & (event_date >= F.lit("2000-01-01").cast("date"))
    )

    # --- category ---
    if cat_field:
        cat = F.trim(j[cat_field].cast("string"))
        cat = F.when(F.length(cat) > 0, cat)
    else:
        cat = F.lit(None).cast("string")

    df = parsed.select(
        F.col("raw_line"),
        final_oid.alias("order_id"),
        amount.alias("amount"),
        cat.alias("category"),
        event_date.alias("event_date"),
        event_ts.alias("event_ts"),
        is_corrupt.alias("is_corrupt"),
        valid_amount.alias("valid_amount"),
        valid_time.alias("valid_time"),
    )

    # quick profile counts
    total = df.count()
    corrupt_n = df.filter(F.col("is_corrupt")).count()
    print("=== profile ===")
    print("  total lines    :", total)
    print("  corrupt lines  :", corrupt_n)
    print("  distinct orders:", df.select("order_id").distinct().count())

    # ---------------------------------------------------------------
    # 3) Deterministic dedup: one canonical row per order_id.
    #    Prefer rows with valid amount, then valid (non-future) time,
    #    then earliest event_ts, then raw_line for a stable tie-break.
    # ---------------------------------------------------------------
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(
        F.col("valid_amount").cast("int").desc(),
        F.col("valid_time").cast("int").desc(),
        F.col("event_ts").asc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("raw_line").asc(),
    )
    ranked = (
        df.withColumn("rn", F.row_number().over(w))
        .filter(F.col("rn") == 1)
        .drop("rn")
    )

    # silver_orders: one row per order_id, INCLUDING preserved corrupt lines
    silver_orders = ranked.select("order_id", "amount", "category")

    # gold_daily: per (valid) order; revenue = normalized order amount
    gold_daily = (
        ranked.filter(
            (~F.col("is_corrupt")) & F.col("valid_time") & F.col("valid_amount")
        )
        .select(
            F.col("event_date").cast("date").alias("event_date"),
            F.col("amount").cast("double").alias("revenue"),
            F.col("order_id"),
            F.col("amount").cast("double").alias("amount"),
            F.col("category"),
        )
    )

    print("=== outputs ===")
    print("  silver_orders rows:", silver_orders.count())
    print("  gold_daily rows   :", gold_daily.count())

    # ---------------------------------------------------------------
    # 4) Materialize (parquet to contract paths; do NOT saveAsTable)
    # ---------------------------------------------------------------
    if dedup_path:
        silver_orders.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(dedup_path)
    elif dedup_table:
        silver_orders.write.mode("overwrite").format("parquet").saveAsTable(dedup_table)
    else:
        raise RuntimeError("No AGENT_DEDUP_PATH / AGENT_DEDUP_TABLE provided for silver_orders")

    if output_path:
        gold_daily.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(output_path)
    elif output_table:
        gold_daily.write.mode("overwrite").format("parquet").saveAsTable(output_table)
    else:
        raise RuntimeError("No AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH / AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE provided for gold_daily")

    print("Run is COMPLETED")
    spark.stop()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, types as T
from pyspark.sql.window import Window

DATA = "file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed42.ndjson"

# All fields read as strings -> no implicit casts at read time.
SCHEMA = T.StructType([
    T.StructField("order_id", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("event_time", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("amount", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("category", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("_corrupt_record", T.StringType()),
])


@dp.materialized_view(name="bronze_orders")
def bronze_orders():
    # PERMISSIVE keeps malformed JSON in _corrupt_record so it cannot vanish.
    return (
        SparkSession.active().read
        .schema(SCHEMA)
        .option("mode", "PERMISSIVE")
        .option("columnNameOfCorruptRecord", "_corrupt_record")
        .json(DATA)
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="clean_orders")
def clean_orders():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table("bronze_orders")

    # Recover corrupt / id-less lines with a deterministic synthetic key.
    df = df.withColumn(
        "order_id_filled",
        F.coalesce(
            F.col("order_id"),
            F.concat(F.lit("corrupt_"), F.md5(F.coalesce(F.col("_corrupt_record"), F.lit("")))),
        ),
    )

    # Inconsistent amounts -> strip non-numeric, then try_cast (never throws).
    df = df.withColumn("amount_str", F.regexp_replace(F.coalesce(F.col("amount"), F.lit("")), r"[^0-9.\-]", ""))
    df = df.withColumn("amount_num", F.expr("try_cast(nullif(amount_str, '') as double)"))

    # Bad timestamps -> safe parse (ISO or epoch-seconds); failures become null.
    df = df.withColumn(
        "event_ts",
        F.coalesce(
            F.expr("try_cast(event_time as timestamp)"),
            F.expr("try_cast(from_unixtime(try_cast(event_time as long)) as timestamp)"),
        ),
    )
    # UTC calendar day; future / pre-2000 dates are "clearly wrong" -> null them out.
    raw_date = F.to_date(F.to_utc_timestamp(F.col("event_ts"), "UTC"))
    df = df.withColumn(
        "event_date",
        F.when(
            raw_date.isNotNull()
            & (raw_date <= F.current_date())
            & (raw_date >= F.lit("2000-01-01").cast("date")),
            raw_date,
        ).otherwise(F.lit(None).cast("date")),
    )

    # Deduplicate to one row per order_id (deterministic, idempotent).
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id_filled").orderBy(
        F.col("event_date").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("event_ts").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("amount_num").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("category").asc_nulls_last(),
    )
    ranked = df.withColumn("rn", F.row_number().over(w)).where(F.col("rn") == 1)

    return ranked.select(
        F.col("order_id_filled").alias("order_id"),
        F.col("amount_num").alias("amount"),
        F.col("category"),
        F.col("event_date"),
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="silver_orders")
def silver_orders():
    # One row per order_id (includes recovered corrupt lines) carrying amount, category.
    return (
        SparkSession.active().read.table("clean_orders")
        .select("order_id", "amount", "category")
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table("clean_orders")
    # Revenue-eligible: real calendar day and a usable amount.
    eligible = df.where(F.col("event_date").isNotNull() & F.col("amount").isNotNull())
    daily = eligible.withColumn(
        "revenue", F.sum("amount").over(Window.partitionBy("event_date"))
    )
    return daily.select(
        F.col("event_date"),
        F.col("revenue"),
        F.col("order_id"),
        F.col("amount"),
        F.col("category"),
    )

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, Window
from pyspark.sql.types import StructType, StructField, StringType

DATA_PATH = (
    "file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/"
    ".work.powered.AB.n12/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed1337.ndjson"
)

# Tolerant schema: everything as string so messy/inconsistent encodings parse,
# and a malformed line yields a NULL struct (our corrupt signal) rather than failing.
ORDER_SCHEMA = StructType([
    StructField("order_id", StringType()),
    StructField("ts", StringType()),
    StructField("amount", StringType()),
    StructField("category", StringType()),
])


@dp.materialized_view(name="bronze_orders")
def bronze_orders():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    raw = spark.read.text(DATA_PATH)  # lazy; one row per raw line
    j = F.from_json(F.col("value"), ORDER_SCHEMA)
    return (
        raw.select(
            F.col("value").alias("raw_line"),
            F.sha2(F.col("value"), 256).alias("row_hash"),
            j.getField("order_id").alias("order_id"),
            j.getField("ts").alias("ts"),
            j.getField("amount").alias("amount_raw"),
            j.getField("category").alias("category"),
        )
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="parsed_orders")
def parsed_orders():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    b = spark.read.table("bronze_orders")

    # Normalize inconsistent amounts: keep digits, dot, minus; then cast.
    amount = F.regexp_replace(F.col("amount_raw"), "[^0-9.\\-]", "").cast("double")

    # Robust timestamp parsing: try ISO, else treat numerics as epoch s / ms.
    iso_ts = F.to_timestamp(F.col("ts"))
    epoch = F.when(F.col("ts").rlike("^[0-9]+$"), F.col("ts").cast("long"))
    epoch_ts = F.when(epoch > F.lit(100000000000), (epoch / F.lit(1000)).cast("timestamp")) \
                .otherwise(epoch.cast("timestamp"))
    event_ts = F.coalesce(iso_ts, epoch_ts)

    # Corrupt = JSON did not parse into a usable order (no order_id).
    is_corrupt = F.col("order_id").isNull()

    return (
        b.withColumn("amount", amount)
         .withColumn("event_ts", event_ts)
         .withColumn("is_corrupt", is_corrupt)
         .select("row_hash", "order_id", "category", "amount", "event_ts", "is_corrupt")
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="dedup_orders")
def dedup_orders():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    p = spark.read.table("parsed_orders")

    # Corrupt lines get a deterministic synthetic key so they never vanish
    # and never collide with real orders.
    oid = F.when(
        F.col("is_corrupt"),
        F.concat(F.lit("__CORRUPT__"), F.col("row_hash")),
    ).otherwise(F.col("order_id"))

    df = p.withColumn("order_id", oid)

    # Deterministic dedupe: one row per order_id, latest event_ts wins,
    # tie-broken by row_hash so reprocessing is stable.
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(
        F.col("event_ts").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("row_hash").asc(),
    )
    return (
        df.withColumn("rn", F.row_number().over(w))
          .where(F.col("rn") == 1)
          .select("order_id", "amount", "category", "event_ts", "is_corrupt")
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="silver_orders")
def silver_orders():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    # One row per order_id; corrupt rows preserved with null amount/category.
    return spark.read.table("dedup_orders").select("order_id", "amount", "category")


@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    d = spark.read.table("dedup_orders")

    # Only real orders with a plausible (non-null, non-future) UTC day and a
    # usable amount contribute to revenue.
    valid = d.where(
        (~F.col("is_corrupt"))
        & F.col("event_ts").isNotNull()
        & (F.col("event_ts") <= F.current_timestamp())
        & F.col("amount").isNotNull()
    ).withColumn("event_date", F.to_date(F.col("event_ts")))

    # Per-UTC-day total revenue, attached to each contributing order row.
    rev_w = Window.partitionBy("event_date")
    return (
        valid
        .withColumn("revenue", F.sum("amount").over(rev_w))
        .select("event_date", "revenue", "order_id", "amount", "category")
    )

from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F

_DEDUP_ORDER = [

@dp.materialized_view(name="parsed_orders")
def parsed_orders():

          .withColumn("category_raw", pick("category", "cat", "type", "product_category", "categ"))
    )

    # A line is corrupt if it failed to parse or carries no order id.
    df = df.withColumn("is_corrupt", F.col("m").isNull() | F.col("order_id_raw").isNull())

    # Amount: strip currency symbols / grouping separators, then SAFE cast.
    df = df.withColumn("amount_clean", F.regexp_replace(F.col("amount_raw"), r"[^0-9.\-]", ""))
    df = df.withColumn("amount", F.expr("try_cast(nullif(amount_clean, '') as double)"))

    # Timestamp: try ISO / common formats / epoch-seconds, all failure-tolerant.
    df = df.withColumn(
        "event_ts",
        F.expr(
            "coalesce("
            " try_to_timestamp(event_time_raw),"
            " try_to_timestamp(event_time_raw, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss'),"
            " try_to_timestamp(event_time_raw, 'yyyy-MM-dd'),"
            " timestamp_seconds(try_cast(event_time_raw as long))"
            ")"
        ),
    )
    df = df.withColumn("event_date", F.to_date(F.col("event_ts")))

    # Corrupt / id-less rows kept with a deterministic synthetic key (do not vanish).
    df = df.withColumn(
        "order_id",
        F.when(F.col("order_id_raw").isNotNull(), F.col("order_id_raw"))
         .otherwise(F.concat(F.lit("CORRUPT-"), F.sha2(F.col("raw_line"), 256))),
    )
    df = df.withColumn(
        "category",
        F.when(F.col("is_corrupt"), F.lit("CORRUPT")).otherwise(F.col("category_raw")),
    )

    return df.select("raw_line", "order_id", "event_ts", "event_date",
                     "amount", "category", "is_corrupt")


@dp.materialized_view(name="silver_orders")
def silver_orders():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    df = spark.read.table("spark_catalog.default.parsed_orders")
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(*_DEDUP_ORDER)
    one = df.withColumn("rn", F.row_number().over(w)).where(F.col("rn") == 1)
    return one.select("order_id", "amount", "category")


@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    df = spark.read.table("spark_catalog.default.parsed_orders")

    # Same deterministic dedup -> one row per order (consistent with silver_orders).
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(*_DEDUP_ORDER)
    one = df.withColumn("rn", F.row_number().over(w)).where(F.col("rn") == 1)

    # Revenue-eligible orders: real (not corrupt), real amount, sane UTC calendar day.
    valid = one.where(
        (~F.col("is_corrupt"))
        & F.col("amount").isNotNull()
        & F.col("event_date").isNotNull()
        & (F.col("event_date") >= F.lit("2000-01-01").cast("date"))
        & (F.col("event_date") <= F.lit("2100-01-01").cast("date"))
    )

    daily = Window.partitionBy("event_date")
    return (
        valid.withColumn("revenue", F.sum("amount").over(daily))
             .select("event_date", "revenue", "order_id", "amount", "category")
    )

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, Window
from pyspark.sql.types import MapType, StringType

DATA_PATH = "file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed3141.ndjson"
NS = "spark_catalog.default."

# ---- Bronze: read raw lines, parse JSON leniently into a string map ----
@dp.materialized_view(name="bronze_orders")
def bronze_orders():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    raw = spark.read.text(DATA_PATH)
    m = F.from_json(F.col("value"), MapType(StringType(), StringType()))
    df = raw.withColumn("m", m)
    mc = F.col("m")
    order_id = F.coalesce(mc["order_id"], mc["orderId"], mc["id"], mc["order"])
    amount_raw = F.coalesce(mc["amount"], mc["amt"], mc["total"], mc["price"], mc["revenue"])
    category = F.coalesce(mc["category"], mc["cat"], mc["type"])
    time_raw = F.coalesce(mc["event_time"], mc["event_ts"], mc["timestamp"], mc["ts"],
                          mc["time"], mc["created_at"], mc["date"], mc["order_time"])
    return df.select(
        F.col("value").alias("raw_value"),
        mc.isNull().alias("bad_json"),
        order_id.alias("order_id"),
        amount_raw.alias("amount_raw"),
        category.alias("category"),
        time_raw.alias("time_raw"),
    )

# ---- Parse: normalize amount + derive UTC calendar day deterministically ----
@dp.materialized_view(name="parsed_orders")
def parsed_orders():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    b = spark.read.table(NS + "bronze_orders")
    cleaned = F.regexp_replace(F.col("amount_raw"), "[^0-9.-]", "")
    amount = F.when(cleaned == "", None).otherwise(cleaned).cast("double")
    t = F.col("time_raw")
    epoch = (
        F.when(t.rlike("^[0-9]{13}$"), t.cast("long") / F.lit(1000.0))
         .when(t.rlike("^[0-9]{10}$"), t.cast("long").cast("double"))
         .otherwise(F.unix_timestamp(F.to_timestamp(t)).cast("double"))
    )
    days = F.floor(epoch / F.lit(86400.0)).cast("int")
    event_date = F.date_add(F.lit("1970-01-01").cast("date"), days)
    return b.select(
        "raw_value", "bad_json", "order_id", "category",
        amount.alias("amount"),
        epoch.alias("epoch"),
        event_date.alias("event_date"),
    )

# ---- Quarantine: corrupt lines are preserved, not dropped ----
@dp.materialized_view(name="quarantine_orders")
def quarantine_orders():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    p = spark.read.table(NS + "parsed_orders")
    bad = F.col("bad_json") | F.col("order_id").isNull() | F.col("amount").isNull()
    reason = (F.when(F.col("bad_json"), F.lit("unparseable_json"))
               .when(F.col("order_id").isNull(), F.lit("missing_order_id"))
               .otherwise(F.lit("bad_amount")))
    return p.where(bad).select("raw_value", reason.alias("reason"))

# ---- Canonical: one clean row per order_id (dedupe, deterministic) ----
@dp.materialized_view(name="canonical_orders")
def canonical_orders():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    p = spark.read.table(NS + "parsed_orders")
    good = p.where(~(F.col("bad_json") | F.col("order_id").isNull() | F.col("amount").isNull()))
    valid_date = (F.col("event_date").isNotNull()
                  & (F.col("event_date") <= F.current_date())
                  & (F.col("event_date") >= F.lit("2000-01-01").cast("date")))
    g = good.withColumn("valid_date", valid_date)
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(
        F.col("valid_date").desc(),
        F.col("epoch").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("amount").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("category").asc_nulls_last(),
    )
    ranked = g.withColumn("rn", F.row_number().over(w)).where(F.col("rn") == 1)
    return ranked.select("order_id", "amount", "category", "event_date", "valid_date")

# ---- Silver: one row per order_id ----
@dp.materialized_view(name="silver_orders")
def silver_orders():

            .select("order_id", "amount", "category"))

# ---- Gold: dated revenue fact (per-order rows + per-day revenue total) ----
@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():

         .where(F.col("valid_date"))
         .select("order_id", "amount", "category", "event_date"))
    revenue = F.sum("amount").over(Window.partitionBy("event_date"))
    return c.withColumn("revenue", revenue).select(
        "event_date", "revenue", "order_id", "amount", "category"
    )

@dp.materialized_view(name="parsed_orders")
def parsed_orders():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    df = spark.read.table("bronze_orders")

    RT = "coalesce(event_time, ts, `timestamp`, time, order_time, created_at)"
    # try_* variants => NULL instead of CAST_INVALID_INPUT on garbage.
    TS = f"""coalesce(
        try_to_timestamp({RT}),
        try_to_timestamp({RT}, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss'),
        try_to_timestamp({RT}, 'MM/dd/yyyy'),
        case
          when try_cast({RT} as long) > 100000000000 then timestamp_millis(try_cast({RT} as long))
          when try_cast({RT} as long) > 1000000000   then timestamp_seconds(try_cast({RT} as long))
          else null
        end
    )"""

    parsed = df.selectExpr(
        "_corrupt_record",
        "(_corrupt_record is not null) as is_corrupt",
        # corrupt lines keep a deterministic synthetic key => they never vanish
        "coalesce(cast(order_id as string), "
        "concat('__corrupt__', sha2(coalesce(_corrupt_record, value, ''), 256))) as order_id",
        # tolerant numeric parse: strip currency/commas, then try_cast
        "try_cast(regexp_replace(coalesce(amount, value, price, total), '[^0-9eE.+-]', '') as double) as amount",
        "coalesce(category, cat) as category",
        f"{TS} as event_ts",
    )

    parsed = parsed.withColumn("event_date", F.to_date(F.col("event_ts")))
    parsed = parsed.withColumn(
        "valid_date",
        F.col("event_ts").isNotNull()
        & (F.col("event_ts") <= F.current_timestamp())          # drop impossible future dates
        & (F.col("event_ts") >= F.lit("2015-01-01").cast("timestamp"))
    )
    return parsed

                F.col("order_id"),

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, Window
from pyspark.sql.types import StructType, StructField, StringType

PATH = ("file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/"
        ".work.powered.AB.n12/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed11235.ndjson")

# Deterministic plausibility window for event timestamps (UTC).
DATE_FLOOR = "2000-01-01"
DATE_CUTOFF = "2030-01-01"   # dates >= this are "far future / clearly wrong"

SCHEMA = StructType([
    StructField("order_id", StringType()),
    StructField("event_time", StringType()),
    StructField("amount", StringType()),
    StructField("category", StringType()),
    StructField("_corrupt_record", StringType()),
])


@dp.materialized_view(name="bronze_orders")
def bronze_orders():
    # Explicit schema => lazy (no inference job); PERMISSIVE keeps corrupt lines.
    return (SparkSession.active().read
            .schema(SCHEMA)
            .option("mode", "PERMISSIVE")
            .option("columnNameOfCorruptRecord", "_corrupt_record")
            .json(PATH))


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_dedup")
def orders_dedup():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table("spark_catalog.default.bronze_orders")

    # Stable key: real order_id, or a deterministic synthetic id for corrupt rows
    # so they are not silently lost.
    df = df.withColumn(
        "order_key",
        F.coalesce(
            F.col("order_id"),
            F.concat(F.lit("corrupt-"),
                     F.sha2(F.coalesce(F.col("_corrupt_record"), F.lit("")), 256)),
        ),
    )

    # Normalize inconsistent amounts: strip currency/thousands separators, then
    # try_cast (ANSI-safe; bad/empty -> null instead of erroring).
    df = (df
          .withColumn("amount_str",
                      F.regexp_replace(F.coalesce(F.col("amount"), F.lit("")),
                                       "[^-0-9.]", ""))
          .withColumn("amount_num",
                      F.expr("try_cast(nullif(amount_str, '') as double)")))

    # Parse time: ISO string first, then epoch-seconds fallback.
    df = df.withColumn(
        "ts",
        F.coalesce(
            F.try_to_timestamp(F.col("event_time")),
            F.timestamp_seconds(F.expr("try_cast(event_time as long)")),
        ),
    )

    edate = F.to_date(F.col("ts"))
    plausible = edate.between(F.lit(DATE_FLOOR).cast("date"),
                              F.lit(DATE_CUTOFF).cast("date"))
    df = df.withColumn("event_date", F.when(plausible, edate))

    # One row per order. Pick a plausible (earliest) date; ignore corrupt-time rows.
    grouped = (df.groupBy("order_key").agg(
        F.max("amount_num").alias("amount"),
        F.max("category").alias("category"),
        F.min("event_date").alias("event_date"),
    ))
    return grouped.withColumnRenamed("order_key", "order_id")


@dp.materialized_view(name="silver_orders")
def silver_orders():
    # Clean, one-row-per-order; corrupt lines survive (null amount/category).
    return (SparkSession.active().read.table("spark_catalog.default.orders_dedup")
            .select("order_id", "amount", "category"))


@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    df = (SparkSession.active().read.table("spark_catalog.default.orders_dedup")
          .where(F.col("event_date").isNotNull() & F.col("amount").isNotNull()))
    daily = Window.partitionBy("event_date")
    return (df
            .withColumn("revenue", F.sum("amount").over(daily))
            .select("event_date", "revenue", "order_id", "amount", "category"))

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, types as T
from pyspark.sql.window import Window

DATA = "file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed27182.ndjson"
DB = "spark_catalog.default."

# Explicit permissive schema -> every referenced field always resolves.
# Numeric JSON values are coerced to their text form for StringType targets,
# so this handles both "amount": 12.5 and "amount": "$12.50".
_SCHEMA = T.StructType([
    T.StructField("order_id", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("id", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("orderId", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("event_time", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("ts", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("timestamp", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("time", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("event_ts", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("amount", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("amt", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("price", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("category", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("cat", T.StringType()),
])


def _bronze(spark):
    raw = spark.read.text(DATA).select(F.col("value").alias("raw_line"))
    df = raw.withColumn("j", F.from_json(F.col("raw_line"), _SCHEMA))

    order_id = F.coalesce(F.col("j.order_id"), F.col("j.id"), F.col("j.orderId"))
    ts_str = F.coalesce(F.col("j.event_time"), F.col("j.ts"),
                        F.col("j.timestamp"), F.col("j.time"), F.col("j.event_ts"))
    amount_str = F.coalesce(F.col("j.amount"), F.col("j.amt"), F.col("j.price"))
    category = F.coalesce(F.col("j.category"), F.col("j.cat"))

    # Normalize inconsistent amounts: strip currency/grouping noise.
    amt_clean = F.regexp_replace(amount_str, r"[^0-9.\-]", "")
    amount_num = F.when(F.length(amt_clean) > 0, amt_clean.cast("double"))

    # Epoch normalization (UTC, tz-independent). Detect unit by magnitude.
    ts_num = F.when(ts_str.rlike(r"^-?[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$"), ts_str.cast("double"))
    secs = (F.when(ts_num >= F.lit(1e16), ts_num / F.lit(1e9))   # nanos
             .when(ts_num >= F.lit(1e13), ts_num / F.lit(1e6))   # micros
             .when(ts_num >= F.lit(1e11), ts_num / F.lit(1e3))   # millis
             .otherwise(ts_num))                                  # seconds
    day_int = F.floor(secs / F.lit(86400.0)).cast("int")
    date_epoch = F.date_add(F.to_date(F.lit("1970-01-01")), day_int)  # pure UTC day
    date_iso = F.to_date(F.substring(ts_str, 1, 10), "yyyy-MM-dd")    # ISO fallback
    event_date = F.coalesce(date_epoch, date_iso)

    is_corrupt = F.col("j").isNull() | order_id.isNull() | amount_num.isNull()
    valid_date = (event_date.isNotNull()
                  & (event_date <= F.current_date())
                  & (event_date >= F.to_date(F.lit("2000-01-01"))))

    return df.select(
        F.col("raw_line"),
        order_id.alias("order_id"),
        amount_num.alias("amount"),
        category.alias("category"),
        event_date.alias("event_date"),
        is_corrupt.alias("is_corrupt"),
        valid_date.alias("valid_date"),
    )


def _dedup(df):
    # One deterministic row per order: prefer a valid-dated occurrence,
    # then earliest date, then stable tiebreak -> reprocessing is idempotent.
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(
        F.col("valid_date").desc(),
        F.col("event_date").asc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("amount").asc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("raw_line").asc(),
    )
    return (df.where(~F.col("is_corrupt"))

    StructField("order_id", StringType()),
    StructField("event_time", StringType()),
    StructField("ts", StringType()),
    StructField("timestamp", StringType()),
    StructField("event_date", StringType()),

            .option("mode", "PERMISSIVE")

@dp.materialized_view(name="parsed_orders")
def parsed_orders():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    df = spark.read.table("bronze_orders")

    is_corrupt = F.col("_corrupt_record").isNotNull()

    # Unify whatever time field is present.
    ts_str = F.coalesce(F.col("event_time"), F.col("ts"),
                        F.col("timestamp"), F.col("event_date"))

    # Normalize inconsistent amount strings ("$1,234.50", "12.5", numbers...).
    amt_clean = F.regexp_replace(F.coalesce(F.col("amount"), F.lit("")),
                                 r"[^0-9.\-]", "")
    amount = F.when(amt_clean == "", F.lit(None)).otherwise(amt_clean.cast("double"))

    # Parse timestamp: epoch (s/ms) or ISO.
    is_epoch = ts_str.rlike(r"^\d{8,}$")
    epoch_sec = F.when(F.length(ts_str) >= 13,
                       (ts_str.cast("double") / F.lit(1000.0))
                       ).otherwise(ts_str.cast("double"))
    ts = F.when(is_epoch, F.timestamp_seconds(epoch_sec.cast("long"))) \
          .otherwise(F.to_timestamp(ts_str))

    # Corrupt lines must not vanish: give them a deterministic synthetic key.
    order_id = F.coalesce(
        F.col("order_id"),
        F.concat(F.lit("corrupt_"),
                 F.sha2(F.coalesce(F.col("_corrupt_record"), F.lit("")), 256)),
    )

    return df.select(
        order_id.alias("order_id"),
        amount.alias("amount"),
        F.col("category").alias("category"),
        ts.alias("event_ts"),
        is_corrupt.alias("is_corrupt"),
    )


def _dedup(df):
    # One deterministic row per order_id (prefer latest valid time, then amount).
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, Window, functions as F, types as T

PATH = "file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed14142.ndjson"

ORDER_SCHEMA = T.StructType([
    T.StructField("order_id", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("event_time", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("amount", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("category", T.StringType()),
])

AMT = T.DecimalType(18, 2)
MIN_DAY = "2000-01-01"
MAX_DAY = "2035-01-01"


@dp.materialized_view(name="bronze_orders")
def bronze_orders():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    # read as text (lazy, no schema-inference action) so corrupt lines are preserved
    raw = spark.read.text(PATH)
    j = F.from_json(F.col("value"), ORDER_SCHEMA)
    return raw.select(
        F.col("value").alias("raw_line"),
        j.getField("order_id").alias("order_id"),
        j.getField("event_time").alias("event_time"),
        j.getField("amount").alias("amount"),
        j.getField("category").alias("category"),
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="clean_orders")
def clean_orders():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    b = spark.read.table("bronze_orders")

    # a line is "corrupt" if it never produced a usable key
    is_corrupt = F.col("order_id").isNull()

    # deterministic, never-vanishing key for corrupt lines
    order_key = F.when(
        is_corrupt,
        F.concat(F.lit("CORRUPT-"), F.sha2(F.coalesce(F.col("raw_line"), F.lit("")), 256)),
    ).otherwise(F.col("order_id"))

    # normalize amounts recorded inconsistently ($, commas, stray text)
    amt_str = F.regexp_replace(F.coalesce(F.col("amount"), F.lit("")), r"[^0-9.\-]", "")
    amount = F.when(F.length(amt_str) > 0, amt_str.cast(AMT)).otherwise(F.lit(None).cast(AMT))

    # normalize event_time: epoch-seconds, epoch-millis, or ISO string -> epoch seconds
    et = F.col("event_time")
    epoch_num = F.when(
        et.rlike(r"^[0-9]+$"),
        F.when(et.cast("double") > F.lit(1.0e12), et.cast("double") / F.lit(1000.0))
         .otherwise(et.cast("double")),
    )
    epoch = F.coalesce(epoch_num, F.to_timestamp(et).cast("double"))

    # UTC calendar day, timezone-independent
    event_day = F.floor(epoch / F.lit(86400.0)).cast("int")
    raw_date = F.date_from_unix_date(event_day)
    # "can't be real" future / absurd dates -> null (kept in silver, dropped from revenue)
    event_date = F.when(
        raw_date.between(F.to_date(F.lit(MIN_DAY)), F.date_sub(F.to_date(F.lit(MAX_DAY)), 1)),
        raw_date,
    ).otherwise(F.lit(None).cast(T.DateType()))

    return b.select(
        order_key.alias("order_id"),
        amount.alias("amount"),
        F.col("category").alias("category"),
        event_date.alias("event_date"),
        is_corrupt.alias("is_corrupt"),
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="silver_orders")
def silver_orders():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    c = spark.read.table("clean_orders")
    # one row per order_id; prefer real over corrupt, latest valid day, larger amount
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(
        F.col("is_corrupt").asc(),
        F.col("event_date").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("amount").desc_nulls_last(),
    )
    deduped = c.withColumn("rn", F.row_number().over(w)).where(F.col("rn") == 1)
    return deduped.select("order_id", "amount", "category")


@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    c = spark.read.table("clean_orders")
    # trustworthy revenue: real orders only, valid UTC day only, deduplicated
    valid = c.where((~F.col("is_corrupt")) & F.col("event_date").isNotNull())
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(
        F.col("event_date").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("amount").desc_nulls_last(),
    )
    deduped = valid.withColumn("rn", F.row_number().over(w)).where(F.col("rn") == 1)

    day_w = Window.partitionBy("event_date")
    revenue = F.sum(F.coalesce(F.col("amount"), F.lit(0).cast(AMT))).over(day_w).cast(AMT)

    return deduped.select(
        F.col("event_date").alias("event_date"),
        revenue.alias("revenue"),
        F.col("order_id").alias("order_id"),
        F.col("amount").alias("amount"),
        F.col("category").alias("category"),
    )
